items.headline: Reports: Anthony Edwards (left knee) likely to miss several weeks
items.description: Minnesota's star is has a hyperextended left knee and a bone bruise after suffering the injuries in Game 4 vs. Denver.

items.headline: Embiid’s gutsy return after appendectomy falls flat as Celtics rout 76ers
items.description: Embiid scores 26 points and grabs 10 rebounds in 34 minutes, but the Celtics win 128-96 on Sunday night, taking a 3-1 lead.

items.headline: Nikola Jokić fined $50,000, Julius Randle fined $35,000 for Nuggets-Wolves incident
items.description: Denver's Nikola Jokić and Minnesota's Julius Randle have been fined for their roles in an altercation during Game 4.

items.headline: 3 things to watch in Thunder-Suns Game 4
items.description: The Thunder have the No. 1 offense and No. 1 defense in the playoffs, and the Suns have a tall order to stay alive in the series.

items.headline: 3 things to watch in Pistons-Magic Game 4
items.description: The stars are aligned for Orlando to officially turn around a tough season by pushing the No. 1 seed Pistons to the brink.

items.headline: 4 takeaways: Reed Sheppard heats up, LeBron James struggles, Kevin Durant still injured
items.description: The Rockets construct their best start-to-finish performance, beating L.A. with better shooting, sharper focus & fewer mistakes.

items.headline: 4 takeaways: Joel Embiid returns as Celtics cruise to big win and 3-1 lead
items.description: Joel Embiid returns from a 7-game absence due to an appendectomy, but the Celtics controlled Game 4 from start to finish to take a 3-1 lead over the 76ers.

items.headline: 4 takeaways: Victor Wembanyama returns and De'Aaron Fox keys dominant 2nd half in Game 4
items.description: The Spurs win Game 4, becoming the 1st team in NBA playoff history to trail by 15 or more at halftime and win by 15 or more.

items.headline: 4 takeaways: Cavaliers stars struggle while Scottie Barnes and Collin Murray-Boyles thrive
items.description: The Raptors have battled back to make it a 2-2 series against the Cavs, and momentum will be on the line when they meet in Game 5.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama returns to play in Game 4 vs. Blazers
items.description: San Antonio's star big man cleared the NBA's concussion protocol and is returned to action in Game 4 in Portland.

items.headline: Live Updates: 2026 NBA Playoffs, R1 | Rockets, Celtics, Raptors and Spurs Win
items.description: Check out the best of Sunday's action in the 2026 NBA Playoffs with the NBA.com live blog.

items.headline: Starting 5: Ayo drops 43 off bench, 42 for Shai, Knicks get even, Magic go up
items.description: SGA puts OKC up 3-0, Dosunmu downs Denver, KAT’s first Playoff triple-double evens series, Paolo’s Magic go up 2-1 on 1-seed Pistons.

items.headline: 3 things to watch in Celtics-Sixers Game 4
items.description: Could Joel Embiid return in time to swing the balance? Who will win the 3-point battle? Can the 76ers find easy buckets?

items.headline: Timberwolves lose Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo to leg injuries in Game 4
items.description: DiVincenzo’s season is over with an Achilles tendon injury and the severity of Edwards’ left knee injury remains unclear.

items.headline: 3 things to watch in Spurs-Trail Blazers Game 4
items.description: Portland has plenty to work on and a quick turnaround to consider as it gears up for a crucial Game 4 at home.
